title: Probability Foundations
description: Random variables, joint and conditional distributions, expectation, variance, and covariance — the probabilistic vocabulary every later phase of this course is built from.
objectives: Define a random variable and its distribution, and distinguish discrete pmfs from continuous pdfs
Compute expectation and variance, and explain why both are needed to describe a signal's behaviour
Compute covariance and correlation, and explain why zero covariance does not imply independence
Verify numerically that a signal's autocorrelation computed directly matches the value recovered via the discrete Fourier transform
